commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=99b33ae94005d3e90e1ef1b3e7a1ffd4dd192057)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a core SQLite snapshot provider, `openclaw snapshot create|verify|restore|list`, named OpenClaw database targets, docs, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +730, Tests +716, Docs +103. Total +1549 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible for the remaining PR blocker: the PR reads `PRAGMA user_version` before `VACUUM INTO` and then writes that value into the artifact manifest. I did not run a live race repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is concrete, but the PR is a draft protected feature adding public CLI/artifact surface, so contributor repair plus maintainer acceptance is the right next action.

target.title: Add core SQLite snapshot command
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a core SQLite snapshot command for OpenClaw-owned state.

It includes:

- shared SQLite snapshot provider and local artifact repository
- `openclaw snapshot create|verify|restore|list`
- named OpenClaw database targets: `--target global` and `--agent <id>`
- explicit `--db <path>` support for advanced/manual SQLite files
- docs for which files are safe to sync and which live SQLite sidecars should be ignored
- docs that distinguish `openclaw snapshot` from `openclaw backup`

`openclaw snapshot` is the narrow per-database SQLite sync artifact. `openclaw backup` remains the broader `.tar.gz` recovery archive for state, config, credentials, sessions, and optional workspaces.

## Why

The goal from #94675 is not to make hosts copy live SQLite files more carefully. The goal is for OpenClaw to produce a SQLite-safe sync artifact.

This PR establishes that boundary:

- live `.sqlite`, `-wal`, and `-shm` files remain hot local runtime state
- `openclaw snapshot create` materializes a portable artifact directory containing `manifest.json` and `database.sqlite`
- hosts, containers, object-store sync jobs, and backup systems should sync the completed snapshot directory, not the live SQLite file family
- restore hydrates a fresh local SQLite file from the verified artifact before OpenClaw opens it

## Stack

This PR combines the previous core implementation slices:

- former #94694: core snapshot provider proof
- former #94717: core `openclaw snapshot` CLI
- former #94799: named OpenClaw database targets
- this PR: safe-sync artifact and restore proof/docs

Follow-up validation PR: #94967
RFC: openclaw/rfcs#20
Related: #94675, #94646

## Scope

This does not add WAL bundle deltas, object storage upload, scheduling, leases, failover automation, restore-on-boot behavior, or production orchestration. Those remain future phases. This PR ships the local core command and artifact contract.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
OpenClaw can now convert OpenClaw-owned SQLite state into a syncable artifact directory, verify it, copy it independently from the live SQLite files, and restore it into a verified fresh SQLite database. The packaged CLI path proves `snapshot create`, `snapshot verify`, and `snapshot restore` from `openclaw.mjs`, not only direct source-harness calls.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-snapshot-pr1`, Node v22.22.3, `pnpm build` packaged output, `node openclaw.mjs` launcher commands, temporary local SQLite database created with Node `node:sqlite`, local snapshot repository under `.artifacts/snapshot-proof/pr94805`, copied/restored local SQLite target, plus source Vitest harnesses with WAL-mode SQLite databases and named OpenClaw database targets.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `pnpm build`
2. Create a temporary SQLite DB with `PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL`, `PRAGMA user_version = 314`, and rows `before-wal` / `committed-in-wal`.
3. `node openclaw.mjs snapshot create --db <proof-root>/source.sqlite --repository <proof-root>/snapshots --id proof-db --kind operator-proof --json`
4. `node openclaw.mjs snapshot verify <proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id> --json`
5. Create stale restore-side `<target>.sqlite-wal` and `<target>.sqlite-shm` files, then run `node openclaw.mjs snapshot restore <proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id> --target <proof-root>/restore/source.sqlite --json`.
6. Read the restored DB with Node `node:sqlite` and query `SELECT value FROM entries ORDER BY id` plus `PRAGMA user_version`.
7. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/snapshot.test.ts src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts`
8. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/docs.json docs/cli/snapshot.md docs/cli/backup.md docs/cli/index.md`
9. `git diff --check`
10. `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
11. `pnpm docs:check-links`
12. `pnpm docs:list`
13. `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot create ... --json` returned `ok: true`, a snapshot path under `<proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id>`, `database.id: "proof-db"`, `database.kind: "operator-proof"`, `database.userVersion: 314`, artifact path `database.sqlite`, artifact size `8192`, and a `sha256:<64 hex chars>` artifact hash.

Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot verify ... --json` returned `ok: true` and `integrityCheck: ["ok"]` for the same manifest and snapshot path.

Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot restore ... --json` returned `ok: true`, `integrityCheck: ["ok"]`, the same snapshot path, and target path `<proof-root>/restore/source.sqlite`.

Readback from the restored SQLite DB returned:

```json
{
  "rows": [
    { "value": "before-wal" },
    { "value": "committed-in-wal" }
  ],
  "userVersion": { "user_version": 314 },
  "walExistsAfterRestore": false,
  "shmExistsAfterRestore": false
}
```

The created snapshot directory contained exactly:

```json
[
  { "Name": "database.sqlite", "Length": 8192 },
  { "Name": "manifest.json", "Length": 455 }
]
```

Supporting validation passed:

- `pnpm build`
- Vitest passed 3 shards: `src/commands/snapshot.test.ts` (6 tests), `src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts` (7 tests), and `src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts` (6 tests)
- `oxfmt --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm docs:check-links`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `codex review --base origin/main` returned no actionable correctness issues

Observed result after fix:
The packaged CLI created a verified snapshot artifact from a SQLite database, verified the artifact hash and SQLite integrity, restored the artifact to a fresh SQLite file, removed stale restore-side WAL/SHM files, and preserved both expected rows plus `PRAGMA user_version = 314`. The artifact directory exposed the intended syncable contract: `manifest.json` plus `database.sqlite`, with no live SQLite sidecar files as part of the artifact.

The source tests additionally create WAL-mode SQLite databases, produce snapshot artifact directories, verify manifests and hashes, list snapshots, restore into fresh targets, remove stale restore-side sidecars, run SQLite integrity checks, and read expected restored rows. The named-target tests cover both `--target global` and `--agent <id>` while preserving the explicit `--db` path.

What was not tested:
No live gateway runtime, object storage upload, scheduled backup loop, restore-on-boot path, lease/failover path, cross-platform stress, or WAL bundle/delta behavior was tested in this PR. Stress validation is tracked separately in #94967.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
